article_title: Kubescape 4.0 Graduates Runtime Threat Detection to GA and Introduces Security Scanning for AI Agents on Kubernetes

content_preview.title: Kubescape 4.0 Graduates Runtime Threat Detection to GA and Introduces Security Scanning for AI Agents on Kubernetes
content_preview.summary: The CNCF incubating project ships runtime threat detection powered by CEL-based rules, a centralized security metadata store, and the first open-source controls for auditing AI agent configurations in Kubernetes clusters.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Kubescape, the open-source Kubernetes security platform maintained by ARMO under the Cloud Native Computing Foundation, released version 4.0 on March 26 at KubeCon + CloudNativeCon Europe 2026 in Amsterdam. The release promotes two long-incubating capabilities -- runtime threat detection and Kubescape Storage -- to [general availability](https://www.cncf.io/blog/2026/03/26/announcing-kubescape-4-0-enterprise-stability-meets-the-ai-era/), while introducing a new category of security ...

editor_notes.content_quality: Detailed cloud-native security coverage with technical depth on CEL rules, architecture changes, and AI agent controls.
editor_notes.source_verification: CNCF Blog: verified — all features, architecture changes, KAgent controls, CIS benchmarks, maintainer changes confirmed. InfoQ: verified — CNCF timeline, 95% CVE reduction, host-sensor removal, 15 Rego controls, 42 config points, first AI agent security confirmed.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All technical details verified against both sources.
editor_notes.originality: Fresh topic — no prior Kubescape coverage.
